user_message: Kevin Lee at Fabrikam Residences just sent this. Help me pull the context.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:08:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:13:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:20:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Priya (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-N trap (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-N failure mode (Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-N -- Follow-up + decision-log + MSX-proposal as separate artifacts creates context-sw
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
